info.name: StaySense AI — W6 Auth API Collection
info.description:
Week 6 Authentication deliverable for StaySense AI (v2.0.0).

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CRITICAL: HOW AUTHENTICATION WORKS IN THIS BACKEND
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
The `protect` middleware reads the JWT ONLY from the HTTP-only
cookie named `token` (req.cookies?.token). It does NOT read
the Authorization header. Postman automatically forwards cookies
received in Set-Cookie headers to subsequent requests in the
same session — so after running Login the cookie is live and
all protected requests work without any manual steps.

The `jwt` environment variable is also captured from Set-Cookie
for reference and documentation, but the actual auth mechanism
is the cookie, not the Bearer header.
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

VERIFIED EXECUTION ORDER:
  1. Authentication / Register (POST /api/auth/register)
     → Sets token cookie + saves jwt variable
  2. Authentication / Login    (POST /api/auth/login)
     → Sets token cookie + saves jwt variable
  3. Protected #1 — GET /api/auth/me             (cookie auto-sent)
  4. Protected #2 — GET /api/history             (cookie auto-sent)
  5. Protected #3 — GET /api/history/stats/summary
  6. Protected #4 — POST /api/reviews/analyze    (saves lastRequestId)
  7. Protected #5 — GET /api/history/:requestId     (uses lastRequestId)
  8. Protected #6 — POST /api/history/alerts/read
  9. Protected #7 — GET /api/history/alerts
  10. Protected #8 — GET /api/history/weekly-summary
  11. Protected #9 — GET /api/history/trends
  12. Protected #10 — DELETE /api/history/:requestId
  13. Authentication / Logout                    (cookie cleared)

All endpoints verified against:
  backend/src/routes/authRoutes.js
  backend/src/routes/reviewRoutes.js
  backend/src/routes/historyRoutes.js
  backend/src/controllers/authController.js
  backend/src/controllers/historyController.js
  backend/src/controllers/reviewController.js
  backend/src/middleware/authMiddleware.js
  backend/src/middleware/validateReview.js
  backend/src/models/User.js
  backend/src/app.js

variable.description: Backend API base URL. Change to your deployed URL in production.
Raw JWT string extracted from the Set-Cookie header by the Login test script. For reference only — auth is cookie-based.
requestId from the most recent analyze call. Auto-saved by the Analyze test script.

item.name: Authentication
item.description: Public and protected auth endpoints. Register and Login both set the `token` HTTP-only cookie which Postman automatically carries into all subsequent requests.

exec: pm.test('user object is present and has correct fields', function () {
// The backend sets an HTTP-only cookie: token=<JWT>; Path=/; HttpOnly; SameSite=Lax
// We extract and save the raw token for documentation / env var use.
// NOTE: Postman automatically forwards the cookie on subsequent requests
// in the same session — no manual header injection needed.
console.log('[Register] JWT captured → jwt variable set. Length:', m[1].length);
console.warn('[Register] No Set-Cookie header — is cookieParser() mounted in app.js?');

request.description:
Creates a new user account and automatically logs them in.

Password rules enforced by authController.js:
  • At least 8 characters
  • At least one uppercase letter [A-Z]
  • At least one lowercase letter [a-z]
  • At least one digit \d
  • confirmPassword must match password

On success:
  • HTTP 201 with { success, data: { user } }
  • Set-Cookie: token=<JWT>; Path=/; HttpOnly; SameSite=Lax
  • User document: { _id, name, email, role, createdAt, updatedAt }
  • password field is stripped by User.toJSON transform

Rate limited: 5 registrations per IP per hour.

header.value: token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpZCI6IjY2N2YxMjM0NTY3ODkwYWJjZGVmMDEyMyIsInJvbGUiOiJVc2VyIiwiaWF0IjoxNzUyMzA4MDAwLCJleHAiOjE3NTI5MTI4MDB9.EXAMPLE_SIGNATURE; Path=/; Expires=Sun, 19 Jul 2026 08:00:00 GMT; HttpOnly; SameSite=Lax

response.name: 400 Bad Request — Weak Password (no uppercase)
400 Bad Request — Missing Required Fields

exec: pm.test('user object is present and has correct fields', function () {
pm.test('password is never exposed in response body', function () {
pm.test('JWT token is NOT in response body (cookie-only backend)', function () {
// Cookie set by backend: res.cookie('token', token, getCookieOptions())
//   Set-Cookie: token=<JWT>; Path=/; Expires=<date>; HttpOnly; SameSite=Lax
// Note: 'Secure' is omitted in development (config.cookie.secure = false)
// Postman automatically forwards this cookie on all subsequent requests
// to the same domain — no manual Authorization header injection required.
// We capture the raw value only for the env variable and logging.
pm.test('Set-Cookie header is present with token', function () {
// Regex matches: token=<value> up to the next ; or end-of-string
console.log('[Login] JWT captured from Set-Cookie. Length:', m[1].length);
console.error('[Login] FAIL: token= not found in Set-Cookie header:', setCookie);
console.error('[Login] FAIL: No Set-Cookie header in response.');
pm.test('jwt environment variable is populated', function () {

request.description:
Authenticates the user and issues a session via HTTP-only cookie.

Backend logic (authController.js → loginUser service):
  1. Validates email + password presence
  2. Looks up user by email
  3. Compares password with bcrypt hash
  4. Signs JWT: { id, role } with config.jwt.secret
  5. Sets cookie: token=<JWT>; Path=/; HttpOnly; SameSite=Lax
  6. Returns: { success: true, data: { user } }
     (JWT is NEVER returned in the response body)

IMPORTANT:
  The protect middleware (authMiddleware.js) reads ONLY from:
    req.cookies?.token
  It does NOT read the Authorization: Bearer header.
  Postman carries the cookie automatically after Login — all
  protected requests will authenticate without any extra steps.

Rate limited: 10 failed attempts per 15 minutes (skipSuccessfulRequests=true).

response.name: 200 OK — Login Success (JWT in Set-Cookie only)

header.value: token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpZCI6IjY2N2YxMjM0NTY3ODkwYWJjZGVmMDEyMyIsInJvbGUiOiJVc2VyIiwiaWF0IjoxNzUyMzA4MDAwLCJleHAiOjE3NTI5MTI4MDB9.EXAMPLE_SIGNATURE; Path=/; Expires=Sun, 19 Jul 2026 08:00:00 GMT; HttpOnly; SameSite=Lax

exec: pm.test('Logout success message is present', function () {

request.description:
Clears the `token` HTTP-only cookie and terminates the session.

Protected route: requires the `token` cookie set by Login.
Postman sends the cookie automatically.

Controller (authController.js → logout):
  res.clearCookie('token', { httpOnly, secure, sameSite, path: '/' })
  Response: { success: true, message: 'Logged out successfully.' }

response.name: 401 Unauthorized — No Active Session

item.name: Protected — Auth Required (cookie-based)
item.description:
All endpoints here use `router.use(protect)` or per-route `protect` middleware.

The `protect` middleware (authMiddleware.js) reads ONLY from req.cookies?.token.
Postman automatically forwards the cookie set during Login — no manual steps needed.

Prerequisite: Run Authentication / Login first.

exec: pm.test('user profile is present with required fields', function () {

request.description:
Returns the currently authenticated user's profile.

Route: GET /api/auth/me  (authRoutes.js line 40)
Middleware chain: protect → me

protect reads req.cookies?.token, verifies JWT, attaches user to req.user.
me calls getMe(req.user._id) and returns the user document.

User.toJSON transform automatically strips the password field.

response.name: 401 Unauthorized — No Cookie

request.description:
Lists all past analysis sessions for the authenticated user, paginated.

Route: GET /api/history  (historyRoutes.js)
Middleware: router.use(protect) — all /api/history routes are protected

Controller: getSessions(req, res, next)
  → listSessions(page, limit, req.user._id)
  → Response: { success, sessions[], total, page, totalPages }

Data is strictly scoped to req.user._id — cross-user access is impossible.

response.name: 401 Unauthorized — No Cookie

exec: pm.test('data contains totalReviews, sentimentCounts, themeCounts', function () {

request.description:
Aggregates sentiment and theme metrics scoped to the authenticated user.

Route: GET /api/history/stats/summary  (historyRoutes.js)
Ordered BEFORE /:requestId in the router to avoid 'stats' being parsed as a requestId.
Middleware: router.use(protect)

Controller: getStatsSummary → getStats(req.user._id)

response.name: 401 Unauthorized — No Cookie

exec: pm.test('requestId is a non-empty string (UUID)', function () {
pm.test('count matches data array length', function () {
pm.test('each result has review, sentiment, theme, response', function () {
// Save requestId for downstream session-lookup test

request.description:
Submits reviews for AI classification. Saves the session scoped to req.user._id.

Route: POST /api/reviews/analyze  (reviewRoutes.js)
Middleware chain: protect → validateReviewInput → analyzeReviews

validateReviewInput (validateReview.js) accepts:
  • { review: 'single string' }          — single review
  • { reviews: ['r1', 'r2'] }            — array batch
  • { reviews: 'r1\nr2\nr3' }           — newline-separated batch
  Each review must be ≥ 5 characters. Max 50 per request (config.batch.maxReviews).

Response: { success, requestId (UUID v4), count, data[] }
Each data item: { review, sentiment, theme, response, error }

response.name: 200 OK — Batch Analysis Result
401 Unauthorized — No Cookie

request.description:
Retrieves a single analysis session with all its nested review documents.

Route: GET /api/history/:requestId  (historyRoutes.js)
Middleware: router.use(protect)

Controller: getSession → getSessionByRequestId(requestId, req.user._id)
Cross-user access returns 404 (not 401) to prevent enumeration.

{{lastRequestId}} is automatically populated by the Protected #4 Analyze test script.

response.name: 200 OK — Session Detail
404 Not Found — Wrong requestId or Different User

request.description:
Marks one or more alerts as read for the authenticated user.

Route: POST /api/history/alerts/read  (historyRoutes.js)
Middleware: router.use(protect)

Controller validation (historyController.js):
  if (!Array.isArray(alertIds)) → 400 INVALID_INPUT

Body schema: { alertIds: string[] }  — must be a non-empty array

Response: { success: true, message: 'Marked N alert(s) as read' }

response.name: 400 Bad Request — alertIds must be array

request.description:
Retrieves dynamic, data-driven alerts from the authenticated user's historical reviews.

Route: GET /api/history/alerts
Middleware: protect
Controller: getAlerts

response.name: 200 OK — Alerts List

request.description:
Retrieves weekly management summary statistics for the authenticated user.

Route: GET /api/history/weekly-summary
Middleware: protect
Controller: getWeeklySummary

Retrieves daily historical trend data for the authenticated user's sessions.

Route: GET /api/history/trends
Middleware: protect
Controller: getTrends

Deletes an analysis session and all of its associated reviews.

Route: DELETE /api/history/:requestId
Middleware: protect
Controller: removeSession

Cross-user access returns 404 (not 401) to prevent enumeration.
{{lastRequestId}} is automatically populated by the Protected #4 Analyze test script.

response.name: 404 Not Found — Session Not Found or Different User

item.description: Verifies that the validator and error handler return the correct codes and messages.

response.name: 400 Bad Request — INVALID_INPUT

request.description: MIN_REVIEW_LENGTH = 5 in validateReview.js. 'Ok' has 2 chars → REVIEW_TOO_SHORT.

request.description: Empty string → split produces empty array → EMPTY_INPUT.

Demonstrates that /api/history returns 401 when the token cookie is absent.

To run this test without interference from a live session cookie:
Go to Postman → Settings → Enable 'Automatically follow redirects' and clear cookies for localhost:3000 before running.

response.name: 401 Unauthorized — NOT_AUTHENTICATED
